title: Crispy Floral Peruvian Corn Hash
description: A lazy‑Sunday Peruvian side that combines dried white corn, smoky spices, and fresh herbs into a crunchy, paleo‑friendly hash bursting with floral accents.
instructions: Rinse the dried corn kernels under cold water and pat them dry with a clean towel.
Soak the corn in warm water for 20–30 minutes to soften, then drain and pat dry again to ensure crispness.
Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat until shimmering.
Add the corn kernels, spreading them out in an even layer; let them cook without stirring for 5 minutes until golden brown spots form.
Turn the kernels, add the remaining oil, smoked paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper; continue cooking for 4–5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until all kernels are crisp.
Reduce the heat to medium and add the sliced green onions; sauté for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Sprinkle the chopped cilantro and optional oregano over the corn; toss to combine and cook for an additional 30 seconds.
Remove from heat, taste, and adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve the hash hot, either on its own or over a bed of fresh greens.
